sourceMovie.title: Finding Dory
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Finding Dory for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Andrew Stanton keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Finding Dory clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Finding Dory clicked for you, The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Finding Dory?
faq.answer: Try Moana, The Super Mario Bros. Movie, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs—each shares DNA with Finding Dory while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Finding Dory?
faq.answer: Finding Dory (2016) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Finding Dory part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Finding Nemo Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
